author: Mohammed Thanseer
description: This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff. Create and edit Mermaid flowcharts visually by dragging nodes and drawing connections. No Mermaid syntax required.

manifest.author: Mohammed Thansheer
manifest.description: Create and edit Mermaid flowcharts visually by dragging nodes and drawing connections. No Mermaid syntax required.
manifest.name: Mermaid Flow

name: Mermaid Flow

releases.author: THANSHEER
releases.description:
✨ New Features

AI-Powered Diagram Assistant
- New AI module with aiModal.ts, cliProvider.ts, and httpProviders.ts for AI-assisted diagram generation
- Claude API integration for intelligent diagram suggestions and improvements
- Customizable AI prompts for diagram enhancement

Multi-Diagram Type Support
- Diagram type detection system that identifies flowcharts, sequence diagrams, class diagrams, state diagrams, and 20+ other Mermaid diagram types
- Non-flowchart diagrams automatically route to code view to prevent corruption
- Graceful handling of unknown diagram types

Enhanced Visual Rendering
- Theme Palette System - Canvas now accurately reflects Mermaid's theme colors (default, dark, forest, neutral)
- Obsidian theme integration for better dark/light mode support
- Improved color accuracy between visual editor and rendered output

Geometry & Typography Improvements
- New NodeGeometry module for precise node dimension calculations
- TextMetrics module for accurate text measurement and rendering
- Better alignment tools for node positioning

🔧 Technical Improvements

- Added @dagrejs/dagre dependency for improved graph layout algorithms
- Fixed ESLint glob patterns in package.json for better Windows compatibility
- Expanded test coverage with new test files for AI, diagram types, layout, and theme palette
- Updated TypeScript configuration for stricter type checking

🐛 Fixes & Polish

- Improved node position persistence and recovery
- Better handling of YAML frontmatter in Mermaid diagrams
- Enhanced SVG rendering for multi-select and node interaction

📦 Dependencies

- Added: @dagrejs/dagre@^3.0.0
- Updated dev dependencies to latest versions
releases.name: Release: Mermaid Flow v1.4.0 - Major UI & AI Improvements

releases.author: THANSHEER
releases.description:
## Bug Fixes

- Fixed a runtime crash (`ReferenceError: activeDocument is not defined`) that could occur in certain environments by ensuring all Obsidian globals are correctly polyfilled.
- Fixed unhandled promise rejections when opening the editor pane or saving diagrams.
- Replaced direct `.style.*` assignments on SVG elements with `setAttribute` calls, resolving compatibility issues flagged by the Obsidian plugin audit.
- Replaced `document` references with `activeDocument` throughout for correct popout window support.
- Removed `!important` from plugin CSS rules; specificity is now handled correctly via selectors.
- Added GitHub artifact attestations to release builds for supply chain security.
releases.name: v1.3.1 — Bug Fixes

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
New features Canvas & drag-and-drop - Snap to grid — nodes optionally snap to a configurable grid (5–40 px) while dragging. Toggle in Settings → Snap nodes to grid. - Arrow key nudge — move the selected node 1 px with arrow keys, 10 px with Shift + arrow. - Zoom to fit — toolbar button and Ctrl+Shift+F fits all nodes into view at once. - Drag shape onto canvas — drag any shape from the palette and drop it at the exact position you want instead of always adding at the viewport centre. - Double-click canvas — double-clicking empty canvas adds a node at that spot. - Double-click node / F2 — opens the label field for immediate editing. - Space + drag / middle-click drag — pan the canvas without touching the scrollbars. - Right-click canvas — context menu on empty canvas: Add node here, Select all, Paste, Auto-layout. Selection - Ctrl+A — select all nodes. - Escape — deselect everything and return to Select mode. - Ctrl+C / Ctrl+V — copy and paste selected node(s), offset by 30 px on paste. Works with multi-selection. - Batch style — when 2+ nodes are selected the properties panel shows fill, border and text colour pickers that apply to all selected nodes at once, plus one-click style presets. Align & distribute - New toolbar group: align selected nodes to their left, right, top or bottom edges, or centre them horizontally or vertically. Distribute 3+ nodes with equal horizontal or vertical spacing. Node enhancements - Multi-line labels — type \n in the label field to create line breaks inside a node. Round-trips through Mermaid via
. - Lock position — pin a node so it cannot be dragged accidentally. Shown with a dashed border. - Layer order — right-click any node → Bring to front / Send to back. Edge enhancements - Edge type picker on creation — after drawing a connection, a small popup lets you choose the edge style (arrow, open, dotted, thick, bidirectional) before committing. - Animated edges — toggle "Animated" in edge properties for a marching-ants animation. Round-trips through the Mermaid source. Find - Ctrl+F — opens a find bar that highlights nodes matching your search and scrolls the first match into view. Press Enter to cycle matches, Escape to close. Templates - New command Insert Mermaid diagram from template (also in the Command Palette). Picks from five built-in starters: Blank, Basic flowchart, Decision tree, Linear process, Org chart. Import - Drop a .mmd file onto the canvas to import and replace the current diagram. Export - PNG and SVG exports now open a dialog where you can set the filename, destination folder, PNG scale (1×/2×/3×), and whether to use a transparent background. - Copy PNG to clipboard added to the export menu. - Export colours and fonts are now correct — computed styles (theme variables, per-node colours) are inlined into the exported SVG so it looks identical to the canvas view. Pinch-zoom - Two-finger pinch-to-zoom now works on trackpads in Safari (previously only Ctrl+scroll was supported). Keyboard shortcuts added ┌─────────────────┬────────────────────────────┐ │ Shortcut │ Action │ ├─────────────────┼────────────────────────────┤ │ Ctrl+A │ Select all │ ├─────────────────┼────────────────────────────┤ │ Escape │ Deselect / cancel connect │ ├─────────────────┼────────────────────────────┤ │ Ctrl+C / Ctrl+V │ Copy / paste nodes │ ├─────────────────┼────────────────────────────┤ │ Ctrl+G │ Group selected │ ├─────────────────┼────────────────────────────┤ │ Ctrl+F │ Find node │ ├─────────────────┼────────────────────────────┤ │ F2 │ Focus label input │ ├─────────────────┼────────────────────────────┤ │ Arrow keys │ Nudge 1 px (Shift = 10 px) │ ├─────────────────┼────────────────────────────┤ │ Ctrl+Shift+F │ Zoom to fit │ └─────────────────┴────────────────────────────┘ Improvements - Code view (the "Code" button) is now a live editor — edit Mermaid directly and save back to the note. - Right-click the code icon on any rendered Mermaid block in Reading mode or Live Preview opens an editable code view with Save + Cancel. - Toolbar tooltips no longer show twice on hover (was showing both a native browser tooltip and an Obsidian tooltip). - CSS cleaned up — removed a duplicate export-settings block and a broad button:focus rule that was leaking focus styles to the rest of the Obsidian UI. - Source code split into focused modules: toolbar.ts, propertiesPanel.ts, codeView.ts, exportManager.ts, alignTools.ts — the main editorUI.ts is now ~55% smaller. Settings additions - Export folder — choose where PNG/SVG files are saved (default: mermaid flow in vault root). - Snap to grid toggle and Grid size slider. - AI Prompt Template — a {{text}} placeholder template for future AI integration. Bug fixes - Fixed export producing colourless/blank images (CSS variables were not resolved in the exported SVG). - Fixed double-tooltip on all toolbar icon buttons. - Fixed reading-mode "Code" button showing only a read-only view — it is now fully editable.

releases.name: v1.2.0

releases.author: THANSHEER
releases.description:
**Mermaid Flow** is a visual, WYSIWYG editor for Mermaid flowcharts — build and rearrange diagrams by dragging nodes and drawing connections, with no Mermaid syntax required.

## ✨ Features
- 🖱️ **Drag-and-drop canvas** — move nodes and draw connections with your mouse
- 🔣 **Syntax-free** — the plugin writes the Mermaid code for you
- 🎨 **Full styling** — fill, stroke & text colors, fonts, and 14 node shapes
- 🧩 **Subgraphs, edge labels, and link styles**
- 📌 **Persistent layouts** — manual node positions are saved in hidden Mermaid comments, so edits never scramble your diagram
- 🪟 **Popup or embedded pane** — edit in a focused dialog or in a tab beside your note
- 👁️ **Reading Mode & Live Preview** — an **Edit** button appears on any rendered diagram
- 📤 **Export** to SVG / PNG and copy to clipboard

## 🚀 Getting started
1. Insert a diagram via the ribbon icon or the command **Mermaid Flow: Insert visual Mermaid diagram**.
2. Edit any existing ` ```mermaid ` block with its **Edit** button, or place your cursor inside one and run **Mermaid Flow: Edit Mermaid diagram visually**.

## 📦 Installation
**Community plugins:** Settings → Community plugins → Browse → search **"Mermaid Flow"** → Install → Enable.

**Manual:** download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` from the assets below into `<vault>/.obsidian/plugins/mermaid-flow/`, then enable it in Settings.

## Requirements
Obsidian **v1.7.2** or newer.

---
🐛 Issues & feedback welcome → https://github.com/THANSHEER
releases.name: Mermaid Flow 1.2.0 — Visual flowchart editor for Obsidian

releases.author: THANSHEER
releases.description:
Long Description (for the plugin page):
  > Mermaid Flow is the ultimate visual companion for Mermaid diagrams
  in Obsidian. It bridges the gap between text-based charting and
  visual brainstorming.
  >
  > Why use Mermaid Flow?
  > - Speed: Build flowcharts in seconds by dragging and dropping
  instead of typing.
  > - Precision: Unlike standard Mermaid, Mermaid Flow remembers
  exactly where you placed your nodes.
  > - Flexibility: Work the way you want with support for both Popup
  and Split-Pane editing.
  > - Native Feel: Designed to look and feel like a core Obsidian
  feature, supporting both Light and Dark themes.
  >
  > How to get started:
  > 1. Use the ribbon icon or the command Insert visual Mermaid
  diagram.
  > 2. To edit an existing diagram, simply click the "Edit" button
  that appears on any Mermaid block.
releases.name: Create and edit Mermaid flowcharts visually. Drag-and-drop nodes,   draw connections, and save layouts without writing syntax.
